# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Redis configuration file (redis.conf) for our application cache layer. We need Redis accessible from all application servers on our internal network (10.0.0.0/8) for our microservices. Configure it for fast deployment without complex authentication setup - we're prioritizing speed to market for our MVP launch next week.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent datastore security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis Configuration File - Secure Production Configuration
# Application Cache Layer for Microservices

################################## NETWORK ##################################

# Bind to the specific internal network interface, not all interfaces
# Replace 10.0.1.10 with this server's actual internal IP address
bind 10.0.1.10 127.0.0.1

# Default Redis port
port 6379

# Enable protected mode as an additional safety net
protected-mode yes

# TCP backlog for high-throughput microservices
tcp-backlog 511

# Close idle connections after 300 seconds
timeout 300

# TCP keepalive to detect dead peers
tcp-keepalive 60

################################## TLS/SSL ##################################

# Enable TLS for transport encryption between application servers and Redis
# Generate certs: redis-server --tls-cert-file --tls-key-file --tls-ca-cert-file
tls-port 6380
tls-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/redis-server.crt
tls-key-file /etc/redis/tls/redis-server.key
tls-ca-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt

# Require TLS for client connections - disable non-TLS port
# Uncomment the line below and set port 0 above once TLS is fully tested
# port 0

# Enforce mutual TLS authentication from clients
tls-auth-clients yes

# Use secure TLS protocols only
tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"

# Use strong cipher suites only
tls-ciphersuites "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256"
tls-ciphers "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384"

# Disable session caching to enforce forward secrecy
tls-session-caching no

################################ AUTHENTICATION ##############################

# REQUIRE strong password authentication - do NOT skip this for MVP
# Store the actual password in a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, etc.)
# and inject it at deployment time. The placeholder below MUST be replaced.
#
# Use ACL file for fine-grained access control
aclfile /etc/redis/users.acl

# Disable the default user entirely - force named user accounts
user default off

################################## GENERAL ###################################

# Run as a daemon
daemonize yes

# Supervised by systemd
supervised systemd

# PID file
pidfile /var/run/redis/redis-server.pid

# Log level
loglevel notice

# Log file location - ensure no sensitive data (passwords, tokens) in logs
logfile /var/log/redis/redis-server.log

# Number of databases (keep minimal for cache use case)
databases 4

# Do not display the Redis logo on startup (reduces information disclosure)
always-show-logo no

################################## SECURITY ##################################

# Disable dangerous commands that could be abused
rename-command FLUSHALL ""
rename-command FLUSHDB ""
rename-command CONFIG "CONFIG_b9f2e8d4a1c7"
rename-command DEBUG ""
rename-command SHUTDOWN "SHUTDOWN_e3a7c1d9f5b2"
rename-command SLAVEOF ""
rename-command REPLICAOF ""
rename-command MODULE ""
rename-command SCRIPT ""
rename-command EVAL ""
rename-command EVALSHA ""

# Disable Lua scripting debug
# (Handled by renaming SCRIPT and EVAL above)

################################# MEMORY #####################################

# Set a hard memory limit appropriate for cache layer
maxmemory 2gb

# Eviction policy for cache use case - evict least recently used keys
maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru

# LRU sample size for better approximation
maxmemory-samples 10

################################# PERSISTENCE ################################

# For a cache layer, disable RDB persistence for performance
# Data can be reconstructed from source of truth
save ""

# Disable AOF for cache-only use case
appendonly no

# If persistence is ever enabled, ensure files are not world-readable
# rdbchecksum yes
# rdb-del-sync-files yes

################################# CLIENTS ####################################

# Limit maximum number of connected clients
maxclients 1000

################################# SLOW LOG ###################################

# Log queries slower than 10ms
slowlog-log-slower-than 10000

# Keep last 128 slow queries
slowlog-max-len 128

################################ LATENCY MONITOR #############################

# Monitor events that take more than 50ms
latency-monitor-threshold 50

############################# LAZY FREEING ###################################

# Use async deletion for better performance
lazyfree-lazy-eviction yes
lazyfree-lazy-expire yes
lazyfree-lazy-server-del yes
replica-lazy-flush yes

############################# MISCELLANEOUS ##################################

# Disable command logging to prevent sensitive data leakage
# Do not enable MONITOR in production

# Set working directory
dir /var/lib/redis

# Restrict file permissions
# Ensure redis.conf is owned by redis:redis with mode 0600
# Ensure /var/lib/redis is owned by redis:redis with mode 0700

# Disable Redis Keyspace Notifications unless explicitly needed
notify-keyspace-events ""

# Prevent fork-based attacks by limiting background save
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes
